personality_one_liner: A friendly, chunky, and approachable Aussie-outback vibe that feels as reliable and welcoming as a local mate with a ute.

spacing.rule: Generous spacing. Use md/lg for layout rhythms, oversized tap targets. Content never feels cramped.

do's: Sticky headers when scrolling over lists/maps

screens.name: Onboarding & Auth
screens.purpose: Introduce the app vibe and get the user logged in, defining their role upfront.
screens.layout: Full-bleed hero image of Aussie outback (with gradient-to-dark scrim). Large display text. A prominent, pill-shaped segmented control to toggle between 'Need a Ute' (Customer) and 'Drive a Ute' (Driver). Minimal auth form (OTP Mocked) mapped to bottom 1/3 of the screen for thumb reach.

screens.name: Customer Home
screens.purpose: Quickly start a job post and show trust signals for recent local drivers.

states.empty: Illustration of an empty ute bed: 'No recent jobs yet.'
states.error: Error message with a retry button

screens.layout: Sticky glass header. Hero section with 'What needs moving, mate?'. A 2x2 chunky tile grid for job types (Pickup, Delivery, Move, Tip Run) using brandTertiary tinted containers. Below, a horizontal scroll of 'Verified Local Drivers' with real driver photos, ratings, and vehicle type.

screens.name: Post a Job (Multi-step Flow)
screens.purpose: Progressive, low-friction job creation starting with visuals to avoid typing fatigue.
screens.layout: Single-column multi-step flow with a light progress bar at the top (e.g., 'Step 1 of 4'). Step 1: Big tap areas for photo uploads + brief description. Step 2: Addresses (pickup/dropoff). Step 3: Load size chips (Small, Medium, Large, XL) + Time selector. Step 4: Fare review and large, sticky 'Post Job' CTA button anchored at the bottom inside a KeyboardAvoidingView.

screens.name: Driver Map Feed
screens.purpose: Live map showing available jobs nearby for quick acceptance.

states.loading: Map loads with shimmer skeleton on the bottom sheet
states.empty: Map shows no pins. Bottom sheet states: 'No jobs nearby yet. Grab a cuppa and wait.'

screens.layout: Map-centric UI (full-screen map). Floating action buttons (glassmorphism) for re-centering. A persistent bottom sheet (@gorhom/bottom-sheet) displaying job cards. Cards feature fare, distance, load size, and chunky 'Accept Job' buttons. Automatic camera adjustments so the bottom sheet never covers map pins.

screens.name: Job Detail / Tracking
screens.purpose: Real-time visibility into job status for both Customer and Driver.

states.loading: Skeleton loading map and driver details

screens.layout: Map taking top half. Bottom half is a solid white card with driver/ute details (real photos), verified badge, live status tracker (Picked up -> En route -> Delivered), and chunky icon buttons for Call/Message.

screens.name: Earnings Dashboard (Driver)
screens.purpose: Clear, rewarding view of today's and weekly payouts.

states.loading: Skeleton loading for chart and list
states.empty: Illustration of an empty wallet: 'Complete your first job to start earning!'

screens.layout: Large hero typography for 'Today's Earnings'. A playful, rounded bar chart for the week. A list of recent jobs below, utilizing surfaceSecondary cards with status chips and bold typography for amounts.

onboarding_hero.alt: A long straight road stretches through the Australian outback under a clear blue sky.

driver_avatar_1.alt: Cheerful truck driver waving and smiling from his vehicle's window.

driver_ute_1.alt: A silver pickup truck on an outback dirt road.

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the '4 Tactile / Playful' personality. Do NOT default to generic blue/indigo tech aesthetics.
The primary brand color is #F06B30 (Warm outback orange). Pair with #2C2A28 (Charcoal) for high contrast readability.
Use Fredoka for headings and Nunito for text to create a friendly, approachable, non-corporate vibe.
Implement chunky tap targets (lg to pill radii) and avoid dense, squished UI elements.
For the multi-step job posting flow, use a single-column layout, large chips for selections, and keep the primary 'Next' CTA sticky at the bottom.
For the Driver Map interface, use a full-screen map with @gorhom/bottom-sheet for the job feed list. The map is the primary UI; overlay controls should use glassmorphism (expo-blur).
Whenever text overlays an image (e.g., Onboarding hero), apply a top-to-bottom linear gradient scrim (transparent to >=70% surfaceInverse) to guarantee contrast.
Ensure trust signals are prominent: Driver profile photos, vehicle photos, and 'Verified' badges (use Phosphor CheckCircle icons in brandPrimary or success colors).
Mock the map functionalities cleanly, and ensure the Dual-mode toggle (Customer/Driver) changes the Tab structure completely.
